title: Drift Detector report (drift.json)
description: The canonical machine-readable output of a Drift Detector scan. drift.md and dashboard.html are verified projections of this document; drift-scan verify checks that they agree. Structural conformance is checked here; the SEMANTIC invariants (a tile count equals the rows it summarizes, no two findings render identically) live in agent/lib/verify.py, because JSON Schema cannot express them.

schemaVersion.description: The contract this document conforms to. A breaking change bumps this.

generated.description: The scan date (YYYY-MM-DD), passed in as `now` — never wall-clock, so output stays deterministic.

counts.description: Summary tallies. Each is DERIVED from the arrays below and must equal them — that equality is enforced by verify.py, not by this schema.

unaudited.description: Vendors called but whose retirement list is unchecked (UNAUDITED or STALE).

actions.description: One remediation job per (repo, and — for a sunset — the retiring operation/path). NOT one per finding: 30 CVEs against a package are one upgrade.

unit.description: For a sunset, the exact thing retiring — operation, apiPath, host, or version. This is what keeps twelve dead calls from collapsing into one row.

href.description: A pinned blob permalink, or null when the source host is unknown.

catalog.description: Per-vendor catalog-coverage verdicts — whether anyone has checked this vendor's retirement list. A vendor with call-sites and no attestation is UNAUDITED, which is why '0 findings' is not the same as 'clean'.

coverageGrades.description: Per-repo shape coverage — whether the scan could read the repo's calls at all.
